channel.title: Git :: A. Onfroy
channel.description: Remove sensitive files in Git history [Tutorial] Split a GitHub repository into a main repos and a submodule

item.title: Remove sensitive files in git history
item.description: Source: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/6403601/purging-file-from-git-repo-failed-unable-to-create-new-backup Filter the branch to remove the sensitive files/folders (eg. FILE1 and FOLDER1) from history: git filter-branch --index-filter 'git rm -rf --cached --ignore-unmatch FILE1 FOLDER1' HEAD Note: I donвЂ™t know how to adapt if there are several branches. Push the changes to the branch of interest (eg. main): git push --force --set-upstream origin main From StackOverflow: After filter-branch, Git keeps refs to the old commits around, in case something goes wrong.

item.title: Split a GitHub repository into a main repos and a submodule
item.description: Context We have a local folder MAIN associated with a GitHub repository, also called MAIN. The structure is as follows: MAIN в”њв”Ђв”Ђ 1_dir_A в”‚В в”њв”Ђв”Ђ content_AA в”‚В в”‚В в”њв”Ђв”Ђ file_AAA.ext в”‚В в”‚В в””в”Ђв”Ђ file_AAB.ext в”‚В в””в”Ђв”Ђ content_AB в”‚В в”њв”Ђв”Ђ file_ABA.ext в”‚В в”њв”Ђв”Ђ file_ABB.ext в”‚В в””в”Ђв”Ђ file_ABC.ext в”њв”Ђв”Ђ 2_dir_B в”‚В в”њв”Ђв”Ђ content_BA в”‚В в”‚В в”њв”Ђв”Ђ file_BAA.ext в”‚В в”‚В в””в”Ђв”Ђ file_BAB.ext в”‚В в””в”Ђв”Ђ content_BB в”‚В в”њв”Ђв”Ђ file_BBA.ext в”‚В в””в”Ђв”Ђ file_BBB.ext в”њв”Ђв”Ђ README.md в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .git в””в”Ђв”Ђ .gitignore Goal We want to:
